authorization: owner said 'do all lanes until completed' -> Design A implementation attempted
attempt: Design A reachable form: in-graph opaque append (kv_append_node) into the fp16 cache + @function bypass + owned tile reads the opaque-appended cache (no full-MAXC materialization).

result.layer_isolation: BAKES even at NL=1 (one layer) with a REAL canonical-store prefill, while gqa/materialized path gives finite distinct tokens at NL=1/2/4. The failure is NOT multi-layer composition.
result.isolated_trigger: the in-graph opaque append on a CANONICAL-STORE-prefilled cache + computed (in-graph) k,v -- distinct from the microbench (assign-filled cache, external k,v). Consistent with the prior buffer-identity finding (canonical-store-prefill leaves state that breaks the in-graph opaque append; survives a cache rebase).

why_pre_graph_form_also_fails: The true Design A (append as a pre-graph runtime side-effect, vLLM-style) is FUNDAMENTALLY INCOMPATIBLE with a transformer: layer N's k,v depend on layer N-1's attention READ of the cache, so appends and reads are INTERLEAVED per layer -- you cannot precompute all appends before the captured graph. vLLM works because CUDA graphs allow in-place buffer mutation that persists WITHIN a replay; tinygrad's @function pure-graph requires materialization for that persistence.
classification: Implementation requires a CORE tinygrad TinyJit/HCQ engine capability: an in-place cache store + subsequent load within the captured graph that persists across replays WITHOUT the pure-@function full-buffer materialization. This is NOT reachable from the model/route layer (proven: bakes at 1 layer), and per the design-scope stop rules ('do not rewrite all TinyJit buffer semantics -> TOO_BROAD') it is a core-engine project, not completable as a model task. The Design A SPEC stands; its implementation is core-engine work.
default_safe: diagnostic route reverted; default decode byte-identical
